human_actions_pending.context: Paper 2 now updated with Ramanujan appendix. Both ready.
Grok confirmed r metric error and 7/8 offset. Shared summary already.

decisions.decision: Similarity transforms (Q^T H Q) cannot bridge GCD and spectral bases
decisions.rationale: Orthogonal transforms preserve eigenvalues; r is purely an eigenvalue-distribution property. Verified with 4 bases + Procrustes.

decisions.decision: Peak-gap r must use Hardy Z-function |Z(m_k)|, not |det(z-eigs)|
decisions.rationale: Old det proxy gave r=0.04 for exact zeros; Hardy-Z gives r=0.88 (N=200). Grok confirmed r=0.73 (different trimming).

decisions.decision: q=1 Ramanujan error coefficient = +0.887 is the 7/8 counting formula constant
decisions.rationale: 7/8 = 0.875, residual +0.012 from arg(Gamma(1/4)) in theta function

decisions.decision: 98% of explicit formula error concentrates at Ramanujan q <= 5
decisions.rationale: Clean arithmetic structure: q=1 (offset), q=3,8,12,14 (highly composite numbers)
Correlation +0.998. This is the trivial character projection in the Hecke algebra.

decisions.decision: More primes/harmonics in explicit formula is a dead end
decisions.rationale: 303->5000 primes: err barely changes. M=5->20: negligible. Series already converged.

decisions.decision: Explicit formula error is systematic, not random — anti-correlates with gap-peak
decisions.rationale: Random noise at err=0.36 gives r=+0.71; exact S(T) at same err gives r=+0.03. Smooth T-dependent error destroys fine structure.

decisions.decision: Analytic continuation from sigma>1 is the correct next path
decisions.rationale: Bypasses the systematic error problem entirely. Session 7 showed smooth eigenvalue trajectories (eig_1: 17.81->14.18, zero at 14.13). Needs spectral completeness proof.

next_action: Pick up the analytic continuation operator from Session 7 (_analytic_continuation_operator.py). The operator H(sigma) is well-defined at sigma>1 with absolutely convergent sums. Eigenvalue trajectories as sigma->1/2 are smooth. The frontier: prove SPECTRAL COMPLETENESS — that the N eigenvalue trajectories land ON the N zeta zeros at sigma=1/2, with no crossings or gaps. Measure Hardy-Z r along the continuation path.
context_notes: Session 8 was the Ramanujan Q hunt. Started trying to find a basis transformation Q between GCD (high r, wrong eigs) and explicit formula (right eigs, low r). Key discoveries: (1) similarity transforms preserve spectrum so Q is dead as originally conceived, (2) the old r metric was wrong (det proxy not Hardy Z), (3) exact zeros have r=+0.88 not +0.04, (4) the explicit formula error has clean Ramanujan arithmetic structure (98% at q<=5, q=1 is the 7/8 constant), (5) more primes/harmonics don't help — series already converged, (6) the error is systematic/correlated and anti-correlates with gap-peak structure so even at err=0.36 the r is only +0.03. The operator problem reduces to eigenvalue accuracy, but the explicit formula path is exhausted. The analytic continuation from sigma>1 is the correct next approach — it bypasses the systematic error by computing eigenvalue trajectories rather than using the first-order S(T)/N'(T) approximation. Paper 2 updated with Ramanujan appendix (5 tables, 2 new references). Venv was recreated (Python 3.14, all deps installed).
